description: This schematic illustrates the geometry and dimensional specifications of flat dog-bone-shaped specimens used for uniaxial tensile and stress-controlled tension-tension fatigue tests of Fe-30.5Mn-8Al-1C austenitic low-density steel. The specimen has a total length of 55 mm, with two end clamping sections of 15 mm length and 7 mm width each, a 15 mm long parallel gauge section with 5 mm width, a transition fillet radius of 13 mm, and a uniform thickness of 2.5 mm. All specimens are machined via wire electrical discharge machining with their loading axis parallel to the rolling direction, followed by mechanical and electropolishing to eliminate surface stress and scratches. This standardized specimen design ensures consistent test conditions for evaluating the tensile and fatigue properties of the studied steel with different κ-carbide precipitation states.
